Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Re: Declaratory Relief And Writ Of Mandate; Request For Permanent Injunction And For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Dec-20-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER DAVID PARENTI'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleading Re: Declaratory Relief And Writ Of Mandate; Request For Permanent Injunction And For Attorneys Fees.

Plaintiff's motion for judgment on the pleadings - the functional equivalent of a demurrer - is denied.

In People ex rel. Becerra v. Sup. Ct. (2018) 29 Cal.App.5th 486, as here, plaintiffs moved for judgment on the pleadings against the state of California. As here, the state's answer denied all material allegations. (Id. at 499.) Judgment was nonetheless granted. The Court of Appeal reversed, holding: "the trial court should have denied judgment on the pleadings for the simple reason that the State had denied all of [plaintiffs'] allegations. The trial court had to accept this denial as true." (Id.)

In Fremont Indemnity Co. v. Fremont General Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 112, as plaintiff requests here, the trial court "erred by taking judicial notice of the proper interpretation and enforceability of a letter" The Court of Appeal reversed, holding: "The hearing on demurrer may not be turned into a contested evidentiary hearing through the guise of having the court take judicial notice of documents whose truthfulness or proper interpretation are disputable." (Id. at 114.) Plaintiff's requests for judicial notice in our case are thus denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
